rationale: Independent re-review after the module-scope hash pin was re-opened by an unrelated DI-34 test appended to tests/acceptance/test_record.py (git diff verified purely additive; the DI-29 test function is byte-identical). (2) entry fields (id, title, path, revision) generated from frontmatter: pinned by the exact entries-list equality; probes `"revision": frontmatter.get("revision")` -> `None` KILLED, `"path": md.relative_to(base)...` -> `md.name` KILLED, and (added this review) `"title": str(frontmatter.get("title","")).strip()` -> `""` KILLED; id is pinned by the same exact equality. (3) written to a data file the DMR index renders from, marked generated: 'GENERATED' asserted in the written file, probe dropping the header from write_text KILLED; deterministic regeneration asserted byte-for-byte (second run output == first). Known equivalent mutant from the prior review (drop `sorted(entries, key=id)`) remains a non-clause: ordering is not in the DI text and glob order coincides with id order in the fixture.
